MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint Declaration Of Patrice L. Goldman
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. PAT GOLDMAN, Esq. of Law Offices of Patrice L. Goldman, APC (tel: 415-460-2880) for Plaintiff.
Atty. KELLY DELVAC, Esq. and Atty. JAMES NIKRAFTAR, Esq. of Miller Barondess, LLP (tel: 310-552-4400) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ASMAHAN ATTAYEB's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint.

Matter not argued / contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to file an amended complaint is denied.

Plaintiff filed this action on June 9, 2022. Plaintiff now seeks to add a claim for defamation against defendant Matthew Mullenweg that accrued on October 15, 2024. (Goldman Decl., Ex. C.) A pleading that seeks to allege facts that occurred after the filing of the original complaint is a supplemental complaint. (See CCP 464(a); Foster v. Sexton (2021) 61 Cal.App.5th 998, 1032 ["A 'supplemental' pleading is used to allege facts occurring after the original pleading was filed. [citation] In contrast, the additional allegations in an 'amended' pleading address matters that had occurred before the original pleading was filed."].) The weight of authority states that it is improper to add a new cause of action in a supplemental complaint. (See Flood v. Simpson (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647 [affirming denial of leave to supplement to add a new claim]; Hebert v. Los Angeles Raiders, Ltd. (1991) 23 Cal.App.4th 414, 426 [same]; Lee v. Bank of America (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 197, 207 [criticizing Honig v. Financial Corp. of America (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 960 [allowing an employee to add a defamation claim that arose after he filed his harassment/termination complaint].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR# 10619 (tel: 925-226-8222 / email: raquel@transcriptlegal.com). =(301/CVA)

Off Calendar May-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF ASMAHAN ATTAYEB AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-600095 WITH CASE CGC-22-600093.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Patrice L. Goldman, Esq., Law office of, (415) 460-2889, for Plaintiff.
James R. Nikraftar, Esq., of Miller Barondess, LLP, (310) 552-4400, for Defendants.

At the outset, the court and counsel discuss a continuance of Plaintiff's pending Motion to Compel which is currently set for hearing in Dept. 301 on May-05-2025. After discussion, the court advises that the above Motion shall be continued to May-06-2025 at 9:00 am in Dept. 301 and counsel should expect confirmation of the continuance via tentative ruling.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as modified. The motion by plaintiffs to consolidate Case No. CGC-22-600093 (Attayeb matter) with Case No. CGC-22-600095 (Westmoreland matter) is denied. This motion was previously decided by the court on December 17, 2024. Plaintiffs present no new material facts or law (CCP 1008(b)) but rather contend that the motion was denied "due to the confusion regarding the notice" of motion and for what purposes consolidation was sought. This is not a change in facts or law that would warrant reconsideration of a prior order. The only changed circumstance Plaintiffs cite is that contemplated summary judgment motions were not filed. But the prospect of a confusing record on summary judgment was one of several reasons this court relied on to deny consolidation; this changed circumstance is not a new material fact warranting reconsideration. In any event. the court is unpersuaded that consolidation is appropriate. The court finds that persuasive the reasoning in the order dated Dec-17-2025 at page two (2), line twenty-three (23) and page three (3), line six (6).

Counsel for Defendant is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the modified tentative ruling as discussed and confirmed at the hearing, and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.
